fundamentals.name: MP Materials Corp.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: MP Materials Corp., together with its subsidiaries, produces rare earth materials in the Western Hemisphere. It operates in two segments, Materials and Magnetics. The Materials segment owns and operates the Mountain Pass Rare Earth Mine and Processing facility located near Mountain Pass, San Bernardino County, California. The Magnetics segment produces magnetic precursor products, including NdPr metal; and manufactures NdFeB permanent magnets. MP Materials Corp. was founded in 2017 and is headquartered in Las Vegas, Nevada.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue but unprofitable with a high valuation and significant operational losses.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 174.6%; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 118.6% YoY — exceptional top-line expansion.
Last quarter EPS surprise of 174.6% beat estimates significantly.
Strong analyst consensus of 'strong_buy' with a high mean target.
Ample liquidity with $1.74B cash and strong current/quick ratios.
negatives: Operating margin -7.9% and profit margin -20.5% indicate unprofitability.
Forward P/E of 56.07x and Price/Sales of 33.23x suggest a very high valuation.
Negative free cash flow of $-174.84M TTM.
High beta of 1.91 indicates significant stock volatility.

news.paragraph: MP Materials is facing headwinds from geopolitical tensions surrounding rare earth elements and a recent partnership with Apple has not prevented a stock decline. Despite a strong earnings beat in the last quarter, the company's valuation remains high with a significant forward P/E and negative operating margins. Insider sales and shifting expectations due to geopolitics are contributing to the stock's volatility, with trading described as strange lately.
